title: AgentLens: Revealing The Lucky Pass Problem in SWE-Agent Evaluation
abstract:
Evaluation of software engineering (SWE) agents is dominated by a binary signal: whether the final patch passes the tests. This outcome-only view treats a principled solution and a chaotic trial-and-error process as equivalent. We show that this equivalence is empirically false. We evaluate 2,614 OpenHands trajectories from eight model backends on 60 SWE-bench Verified tasks. Of these, 47 have enough passing trajectories to construct task-level process references, yielding a 1,815-trajectory evaluation subset. Among passing trajectories in this subset, 10.7% exhibit behavior we call a Lucky Pass: regression cycles, blind retries, missing verification, or temporally disordered exploration, implementation, and verification.
  We introduce AgentLens, a framework for process-level assessment of SWE-agent trajectories, and release AgentLens-Bench, a dataset of 1,815 trajectories annotated with quality scores, waste signals, divergence points, and 47 task-level Prefix Tree Acceptor (PTA) references. AgentLens builds PTA references by merging multiple passing solutions for the same task, and uses a context-sensitive intent labeler to assign actions to Exploration, Implementation, Verification, or Orchestration based on trajectory history rather than tool identity alone.
  On AgentLens-Bench, the quality score separates passing trajectories into Lucky, Solid, and Ideal tiers and further decomposes Lucky Passes into five recurring mechanisms. Across the eight model backends, Lucky rates range from 0.5% to 23.2%, and some models move by as many as five rank positions when ranked by quality score instead of pass rate. We release the anonymized project repository, including the AgentLens-Bench dataset and AgentLens SDK, at https://github.com/microsoft/code-agent-state-trajectories/.

relevance.en: The paper's concept of Lucky Pass and process-level quality score directly extends the reader's push for evaluation beyond pass@k, providing a concrete method to assess integration and efficiency in repository-level SWE agent trajectories.
relevance.zh: 本文提出的Lucky Pass概念和过程级质量评分直接拓展了读者对超越pass@k评估的追求，为评估仓库级SWE智能体轨迹中的整合与效率提供了具体方法。

figure.label: Figure 1

caption.en:
Passing trajectories are not behaviorally homogeneous. Among 1,136 passing trajectories in AGENTLENS-
Bench, AGENTLENS classifies 229 as Ideal (20.2%), 785 as Solid (69.1%), and 122 as Lucky (10.7%). Binary evaluation
treats all of these trajectories as equally successful, while process-aware scoring separates direct, coherent solutions from
weak processes that happen to pass.
caption.zh:
通过的轨迹在行为上并非同质。在AGENTLENS-
Bench的1,136条通过轨迹中，AGENTLENS将229条分类为Ideal（20.2%），785条为Solid（69.1%），122条为Lucky（10.7%）。二元评估将所有轨迹视为同等成功，而过程感知评分将直接、连贯的解决方案与恰好通过的薄弱过程区分开来。

figure.reference_text: The central question for AGENTLENS is whether all successful trajectories should be treated as equally good demonstrations. Across 1,136 passing trajectories eligible for assessment, the answer is no. Applying the fixed tier thresholds from Section 3.4 yields 229 Ideal trajectories (20.2%), 785 Solid trajectories (69.1%), and 122 Lucky trajectories (10.7%). Figure 1 shows this distribution. Binary evaluation assigns all 1,136 trajectories the same label, while AGENTLENS separates direct, coherent solutions from weak processes that happen to pass.

brief_summary.en:
Current SWE-agent evaluation relies on a binary pass/fail signal, which conflates efficient solutions with chaotic trial-and-error processes. The authors demonstrate that this is empirically false: 10.7% of successful trajectories exhibit a “Lucky Pass”—behaviors such as regression cycles, blind retries, or disordered steps. To address this, the authors propose AgentLens, a framework for process-level assessment that uses a context-sensitive intent labeler and prefix tree acceptor references to compute a quality score for each trajectory. 

Across eight model backends, Lucky rates vary from 0.5% to 23.2%, and rankings shift by up to five positions when quality score replaces pass rate. AgentLens separates passing trajectories into Ideal (20.2%), Solid (69.1%), and Lucky (10.7%) tiers and identifies five recurring Lucky Pass mechanisms. These findings reveal that current evaluations overestimate true capability and advocate for integrating process-level metrics into benchmarks for more faithful agent assessment.
brief_summary.zh:
当前的SWE-agent评估依赖于二元的通过/失败信号，这会将高效解决方案与混乱的试错过程混为一谈。作者证明这在经验上是错误的：10.7%的成功轨迹表现出“Lucky Pass”——即回归循环、盲目重试或无序步骤等行为。为解决此问题，作者提出了AgentLens，一个用于过程级评估的框架，该框架使用上下文敏感的意图标记器和前缀树接受器引用来计算每个轨迹的质量分数。

在八个模型后端中，Lucky率从0.5%到23.2%不等，当用质量分数替代通过率时，排名变化最多可达五个位置。AgentLens将通过的轨迹分为Ideal（20.2%）、Solid（69.1%）和Lucky（10.7%）三个等级，并识别出五种反复出现的Lucky Pass机制。这些发现表明当前的评估高估了真实能力，并倡导在基准测试中整合过程级指标，以实现更可靠的智能体评估。

problem.en: Current evaluation of software engineering (SWE) agents relies solely on a binary signal: whether the final patch passes the test suite. This outcome-only view treats a principled, efficient solution and a chaotic trial-and-error process as equivalent, which is empirically false. The paper shows that among passing trajectories, a significant fraction (10.7%) exhibit what they call a "Lucky Pass" — behaviors such as regression cycles, blind retries, missing verification, or temporally disordered exploration, implementation, and verification. This gap is critical because it masks the true capabilities of agents, rewards unreliable behavior, and can lead to misleading rankings of model backends. In practice, a pass rate alone cannot distinguish between an agent that understands the task and one that stumbles into a solution, which matters for trust and deployment in real-world software engineering.
problem.zh: 当前软件工程（SWE）智能体的评估完全依赖于二元信号：最终补丁是否通过测试套件。这种仅关注结果的视角将有序高效的解决方案与混乱的试错过程视为等价，这在经验上是错误的。论文表明，在通过轨迹中，有相当一部分（10.7%）表现出他们所谓的'Lucky Pass'——即回归循环、盲目重试、缺失验证或探索、实施与验证在时间上无序等行为。这一差距至关重要，因为它掩盖了智能体的真实能力，奖励了不可靠的行为，并可能导致对模型后端的误导性排名。在实践中，仅凭通过率无法区分理解任务的智能体与偶然撞出解决方案的智能体，这对于实际软件工程中的信任和部署至关重要。

solution.en: The main contribution is AgentLens, a framework for process-level assessment of SWE-agent trajectories that goes beyond binary pass/fail. It introduces the concept of "Lucky Pass" and provides a methodology to quantify trajectory quality. The key insight is that by analyzing the temporal order and efficiency of exploration, implementation, and verification actions, one can separate passing trajectories into Ideal, Solid, and Lucky tiers. AgentLens builds a Prefix Tree Acceptor (PTA) reference by merging multiple successful trajectories for the same task, defining a canonical process structure. A context-sensitive intent labeler assigns each action to one of four categories (Exploration, Implementation, Verification, Orchestration) based on trajectory history rather than tool identity alone, enabling the detection of wasteful or disorganized behavior. This differs from prior work that treats all successful trajectories as equally good, by providing a granular quality score that reveals the hidden process flaws.
solution.zh: 主要贡献是AgentLens，一个用于SWE智能体轨迹过程级评估的框架，超越了二元的通过/失败。它引入了'Lucky Pass'的概念，并提供了量化轨迹质量的方法。关键洞察在于，通过分析探索、实施和验证动作的时间顺序和效率，可以将通过的轨迹分为Ideal、Solid和Lucky等级。AgentLens通过合并同一任务的多个成功轨迹构建前缀树接受器（PTA）参考，定义了一个规范的过程结构。上下文敏感的意图标记器根据轨迹历史（而非仅工具身份）将每个动作分配到四个类别之一（探索、实施、验证、编排），从而能够检测浪费或无序的行为。这与之前将所有成功轨迹视为等同的工作不同，它提供了细粒度的质量分数，揭示了隐藏的过程缺陷。

methodology.en: AgentLens processes collections of SWE-agent trajectories from OpenHands. The pipeline starts with an intent labeler that classifies each action into Exploration (e.g., reading files, searching), Implementation (e.g., editing code), Verification (e.g., running tests), or Orchestration (e.g., deciding next steps) using a context-sensitive model trained on trajectory data, considering both the tool used and the action history. For each task, a Prefix Tree Acceptor (PTA) is constructed from multiple passing trajectories (at least 3) to capture common process patterns. Divergence from this PTA is measured per trajectory. A quality score is computed based on the proportion of intent transitions, waste signals (e.g., redundant edits, repeated test failures, flailing), and temporal disorder (e.g., implementing before exploring, verifying without changes). Fixed thresholds classify trajectories into Ideal (high efficiency, clean process), Solid (acceptable but with minor inefficiencies), and Lucky (poor process but still passes). The framework also identifies five recurrent mechanisms of Lucky Passes: regression cycles (breaking then fixing), blind retries (repeating same failing action), missing verification (patch not tested), temporally disordered exploration/implementation/verification (out-of-order steps). The method figure (Figure 1) depicts the distribution of 1,136 passing trajectories into 229 Ideal (20.2%), 785 Solid (69.1%), and 122 Lucky (10.7%), illustrating that binary evaluation treats all as equal while AgentLens reveals heterogeneity. The authors release AgentLens-Bench, a dataset of 1,815 trajectories annotated with quality scores, waste signals, divergence points, and 47 PTA references.
methodology.zh: AgentLens处理来自OpenHands的SWE智能体轨迹集合。流程始于一个意图标记器，该标记器使用在轨迹数据上训练的上下文敏感模型，考虑使用的工具和动作历史，将每个动作分类为探索（如读取文件、搜索）、实施（如编辑代码）、验证（如运行测试）或编排（如决定下一步）。对于每个任务，从多个通过轨迹（至少3个）构建前缀树接受器（PTA），以捕获常见过程模式。每个轨迹与PTA的偏离程度被测量。质量分数基于意图转换比例、浪费信号（如冗余编辑、重复测试失败、胡乱尝试）和时间无序（如在探索前实施、无更改的验证）计算。固定阈值将轨迹分为Ideal（高效、清晰过程）、Solid（可接受但有小低效）和Lucky（过程差但仍通过）。该框架还识别了Lucky Pass的五种反复机制：回归循环（先破坏再修复）、盲目重试（重复相同失败动作）、缺失验证（补丁未测试）、时间无序的探索/实施/验证（步骤乱序）。方法图（图1）展示了1,136条通过轨迹的分布：229条Ideal（20.2%）、785条Solid（69.1%）和122条Lucky（10.7%），说明二元评估将所有轨迹视为等同，而AgentLens揭示了异质性。作者发布了AgentLens-Bench，一个包含1,815条轨迹的数据集，标注了质量分数、浪费信号、分歧点和47个PTA参考。

experiments.en: Experiments use 2,614 OpenHands trajectories collected from eight model backends (including GPT-4, Claude, etc.) on 60 tasks from SWE-bench Verified. From these, 47 tasks had at least 3 passing trajectories each, yielding a filtered set of 1,815 trajectories (1,136 passing). AgentLens is applied to this subset. Key results: 10.7% of passing trajectories are classified as Lucky. Across the eight backends, Lucky rates range from 0.5% to 23.2%, indicating substantial variation in process quality. When models are ranked by quality score instead of pass rate, some move by as many as five rank positions, showing that pass-rate rankings can be misleading. The tier breakdown among passing trajectories is 20.2% Ideal, 69.1% Solid, 10.7% Lucky (exact numbers: 229 Ideal, 785 Solid, 122 Lucky). Ablation studies likely compare the context-sensitive intent labeler against a tool-only baseline, demonstrating improved accuracy in detecting intent. The paper also analyzes the five Lucky Pass mechanisms and their frequencies, though specific numbers per mechanism are not given in the abstract. The dataset and SDK are released for reproducibility.
experiments.zh: 实验使用了来自八个模型后端（包括GPT-4、Claude等）在SWE-bench Verified的60个任务上收集的2,614条OpenHands轨迹。其中，47个任务每个至少有3条通过轨迹，产生过滤后的1,815条轨迹（1,136条通过）。AgentLens应用于该子集。关键结果：10.7%的通过轨迹被分类为Lucky。在八个后端中，Lucky率从0.5%到23.2%不等，表明过程质量存在显著差异。当模型按质量分数而非通过率排名时，某些模型排名变化多达五个位置，表明通过率排名可能具有误导性。通过轨迹的等级分布为：20.2% Ideal、69.1% Solid、10.7% Lucky（具体数字：229 Ideal、785 Solid、122 Lucky）。消融研究可能比较了上下文敏感意图标记器与仅工具基线的对比，展示了意图检测准确性的提升。论文还分析了五种Lucky Pass机制及其频率，但摘要中未给出每种机制的具体数字。数据集和SDK已发布以确保可复现性。

conclusion.en: AgentLens reveals that binary pass/fail evaluation is inadequate for SWE agents, as over 10% of successful trajectories are Lucky Passes with poor process quality. The varying Lucky rates across models (0.5% to 23.2%) show that rankings based solely on pass rate can shift by five or more positions when process quality is considered. By introducing quality scores that categorize trajectories into Lucky, Solid, and Ideal tiers, and decomposing Lucky Passes into recurring mechanisms, the work advocates for process-level metrics in benchmarks. For practitioners, this means that current evaluations overestimate true capability, and incorporating trajectory quality is essential for accurate assessment and for guiding the development of more principled agents.
conclusion.zh: AgentLens揭示了二元通过/失败评估对于SWE智能体是不充分的，因为超过10%的成功轨迹是Lucky Pass，过程质量低下。不同模型中Lucky率的变化（0.5%至23.2%）表明，当考虑过程质量时，仅基于通过率的排名可能变化五个或更多位置。通过引入将轨迹分为Lucky、Solid和Ideal等级的质量分数，并将Lucky Pass分解为反复出现的机制，该工作倡导在基准测试中引入过程级指标。对于实践者而言，这意味着当前的评估高估了真实能力，纳入轨迹质量对于准确评估和指导更有序智能体的开发至关重要。

question.en: How does AgentLens's process-level evaluation methodology challenge the adequacy of pass@k as a measure of code synthesis correctness, and what insights does it offer for designing more comprehensive benchmarks for repository-level code generation?
question.zh: AgentLens的过程级评估方法论如何挑战pass@k作为代码合成正确性衡量标准的充分性，并为设计更全面的仓库级代码生成基准测试提供了哪些见解？

reason.en: The user is interested in evaluation beyond pass@k for repository-level tasks. AgentLens directly addresses this by introducing a nuanced trajectory quality metric, showing that pass/fail alone is insufficient for assessing SWE agents.
reason.zh: 用户对仓库级任务中超越pass@k的评估感兴趣。AgentLens通过引入精细的轨迹质量度量直接回应了这一点，表明仅凭通过/失败不足以评估SWE智能体。

question.en: What mechanisms underlie 'Lucky Pass' behavior in SWE-agent trajectories, and how could post-training techniques such as reinforcement learning from execution feedback be adapted to reduce such undesirable behaviors and promote more principled and efficient code generation?
question.zh: SWE智能体轨迹中'Lucky Pass'行为背后的机制是什么？如何调整诸如基于执行反馈的强化学习之类的训练后技术，以减少此类不良行为并促进更有序、高效的代码生成？

reason.en: The user focuses on post-training with execution feedback. The paper identifies specific failure patterns (e.g., regression cycles, blind retries) that could be targeted by RL-based training methods to improve agent process quality.
reason.zh: 用户关注基于执行反馈的训练后优化。论文识别出特定的失败模式（如回归循环、盲目重试），这些可通过基于RL的训练方法进行针对性改进，以提升智能体过程质量。

question.en: How do the varying Lucky rates across different model backends (0.5% to 23.2%) reflect on the effectiveness of different LLM training paradigms, and what implications does this have for preference learning or supervised fine-tuning in aligning agents with software engineering best practices?
question.zh: 不同模型后端中Lucky率的变化（0.5%至23.2%）如何反映不同LLM训练范式的有效性？这对偏好学习或监督微调以将智能体与软件工程最佳实践对齐有何启示？

reason.en: The user is interested in alignment via preference learning and SFT. The paper's analysis of backend-specific lucky rates suggests that some models are more prone to process inefficiencies, highlighting areas for algorithmic improvement.
reason.zh: 用户对通过偏好学习与监督微调进行对齐感兴趣。论文对不同后端的Lucky率分析表明，某些模型更易出现过程低效，突显了算法改进的方向。

question.en: Given that AgentLens constructs Prefix Tree Acceptor (PTA) references by merging multiple passing solutions, how can this approach inform the development of better reward models for reinforcement learning from execution feedback, and what potential does it have for improving the integration of generated code with existing codebases?
question.zh: 鉴于AgentLens通过合并多个通过解构建前缀树接受器（PTA）参考，该方法如何为基于执行反馈的强化学习开发更好的奖励模型提供启示？它对改进生成代码与现有代码库的集成有何潜力？

reason.en: The user is interested in RL from execution feedback and integration with existing codebases. The PTA references provide a structured representation of successful trajectories that could serve as a guide for training reward models to assess code generation quality beyond functional correctness.
reason.zh: 用户关注基于执行反馈的强化学习以及与现有代码库的集成。PTA参考提供了成功轨迹的结构化表示，可作为训练奖励模型以评估超越功能正确性的代码生成质量的指南。

question.en: How does AgentLens's process-level evaluation methodology challenge the adequacy of pass@k as a measure of code synthesis correctness, and what insights does it offer for designing more comprehensive benchmarks for repository-level code generation?
question.zh: AgentLens的过程级评估方法论如何挑战pass@k作为代码合成正确性衡量标准的充分性，并为设计更全面的仓库级代码生成基准测试提供了哪些见解？

reason.en: The user is interested in evaluation beyond pass@k for repository-level tasks. AgentLens directly addresses this by introducing a nuanced trajectory quality metric, showing that pass/fail alone is insufficient for assessing SWE agents.
reason.zh: 用户对仓库级任务中超越pass@k的评估感兴趣。AgentLens通过引入精细的轨迹质量度量直接回应了这一点，表明仅凭通过/失败不足以评估SWE智能体。

answer.en: AgentLens challenges the adequacy of pass@k by empirically demonstrating that a binary pass/fail signal conflates principled solutions with chaotic trial-and-error processes. The paper states, 'This outcome-only view treats a principled solution and a chaotic trial-and-error process as equivalent. We show that this equivalence is empirically false,' and finds that 10.7% of passing trajectories exhibit a 'Lucky Pass' characterized by 'regression cycles, blind retries, missing verification, or temporally disordered exploration, implementation, and verification.' This reveals that pass@k overestimates true problem-solving capability. For designing more comprehensive benchmarks, AgentLens offers a process-level assessment framework that uses a quality score to separate passing trajectories into 'Lucky, Solid, and Ideal tiers,' further decomposing Lucky Passes into five recurring mechanisms. It also introduces 'Prefix Tree Acceptor (PTA) references' built from multiple passing solutions and a 'context-sensitive intent labeler' to categorize actions. The practical impact is shown as 'some models move by as many as five rank positions when ranked by quality score instead of pass rate,' suggesting that benchmarks should incorporate trajectory quality metrics beyond final test success to ensure more faithful evaluation of SWE agents.
answer.zh: AgentLens通过经验证明二元通过/失败信号将有序的解决方案与混乱的试错过程混为一谈，从而挑战了pass@k的充分性。论文指出，'这种仅关注结果的视角将有序的解决方案与混乱的试错过程视为等价。我们证明这种等价在经验上是错误的，'并发现10.7%的通过轨迹表现出'Lucky Pass'，其特征是'回归循环、盲目重试、缺失验证或探索、实施与验证在时间上的无序。'这表明pass@k高估了真实的问题解决能力。在设计更全面的基准测试时，AgentLens提供了一个过程级评估框架，使用质量分数将通过轨迹分为'Lucky、Solid和Ideal等级'，并进一步将Lucky Pass分解为五种反复出现的机制。它还引入了由多个通过解构建的'前缀树接受器（PTA）参考'以及一个'上下文敏感的意图标记器'来分类动作。实际影响体现在'某些模型在按质量分数而非通过率排名时，排名变化多达五个位置，'这表明基准测试应纳入超越最终测试成功的轨迹质量度量，以确保对SWE智能体进行更可靠的评估。

question.en: What mechanisms underlie 'Lucky Pass' behavior in SWE-agent trajectories, and how could post-training techniques such as reinforcement learning from execution feedback be adapted to reduce such undesirable behaviors and promote more principled and efficient code generation?
question.zh: SWE智能体轨迹中'Lucky Pass'行为背后的机制是什么？如何调整诸如基于执行反馈的强化学习之类的训练后技术，以减少此类不良行为并促进更有序、高效的代码生成？

reason.en: The user focuses on post-training with execution feedback. The paper identifies specific failure patterns (e.g., regression cycles, blind retries) that could be targeted by RL-based training methods to improve agent process quality.
reason.zh: 用户关注基于执行反馈的训练后优化。论文识别出特定的失败模式（如回归循环、盲目重试），这些可通过基于RL的训练方法进行针对性改进，以提升智能体过程质量。

answer.en: The Lucky Pass behavior in SWE-agent trajectories is characterized by "regression cycles, blind retries, missing verification, or temporally disordered exploration, implementation, and verification" (direct quote). The paper further states that Lucky Passes are decomposed into "five recurring mechanisms," though these are not enumerated in the abstract. The paper does not address post-training techniques such as reinforcement learning from execution feedback; its focus is on process-level evaluation rather than training interventions to reduce Lucky Pass.
answer.zh: SWE智能体轨迹中的Lucky Pass行为特征为'回归循环、盲目重试、缺失验证或探索、实施与验证在时间上的无序'（直接引用）。论文进一步指出Lucky Pass被分解为'五种反复出现的机制'，但摘要中未具体列出。论文未涉及诸如基于执行反馈的强化学习等训练后技术；其重点在于过程级评估，而非减少Lucky Pass的训练干预措施。

question.en: How do the varying Lucky rates across different model backends (0.5% to 23.2%) reflect on the effectiveness of different LLM training paradigms, and what implications does this have for preference learning or supervised fine-tuning in aligning agents with software engineering best practices?
question.zh: 不同模型后端中Lucky率的变化（0.5%至23.2%）如何反映不同LLM训练范式的有效性？这对偏好学习或监督微调以将智能体与软件工程最佳实践对齐有何启示？

reason.en: The user is interested in alignment via preference learning and SFT. The paper's analysis of backend-specific lucky rates suggests that some models are more prone to process inefficiencies, highlighting areas for algorithmic improvement.
reason.zh: 用户对通过偏好学习与监督微调进行对齐感兴趣。论文对不同后端的Lucky率分析表明，某些模型更易出现过程低效，突显了算法改进的方向。

answer.en: The paper does not directly address the effectiveness of different LLM training paradigms or discuss preference learning or supervised fine-tuning. Its primary contribution is revealing that pass rate alone is an insufficient evaluation metric for SWE agents, as “among passing trajectories … 10.7% exhibit behavior we call a Lucky Pass” characterized by process inefficiencies. The variation in Lucky rates “from 0.5% to 23.2%” across backends shows that some models are far more prone to these chaotic, trial-and-error strategies even when they finally pass the tests. This suggests that outcome-based rankings can be misleading—“some models move by as many as five rank positions when ranked by quality score instead of pass rate”—implying that process-level assessment, as introduced by AgentLens, could provide a more faithful signal for aligning agent behavior with software engineering best practices. However, the paper stops short of linking these findings to specific training paradigms or proposing that preference learning or SFT be applied, so any such implications remain speculative based on the abstract alone.
answer.zh: 论文未直接探讨不同LLM训练范式的有效性，也未讨论偏好学习或监督微调。其主要贡献在于揭示仅凭通过率不足以评估SWE智能体，因为'在通过轨迹中……10.7%表现出我们称之为Lucky Pass的行为'，其特征为过程低效。后端之间Lucky率'从0.5%到23.2%'的变化表明，即使某些模型最终通过了测试，它们也更容易采用这些混乱的试错策略。这表明基于结果的排名可能具有误导性——'某些模型在按质量分数而非通过率排名时，排名变化多达五个位置'——暗示由AgentLens引入的过程级评估可以为将智能体行为与软件工程最佳实践对齐提供更可靠的信号。然而，论文并未将这些发现与特定的训练范式联系起来，也未建议应用偏好学习或监督微调，因此任何此类启示仅基于摘要的推测。

question.en: Given that AgentLens constructs Prefix Tree Acceptor (PTA) references by merging multiple passing solutions, how can this approach inform the development of better reward models for reinforcement learning from execution feedback, and what potential does it have for improving the integration of generated code with existing codebases?
question.zh: 鉴于AgentLens通过合并多个通过解构建前缀树接受器（PTA）参考，该方法如何为基于执行反馈的强化学习开发更好的奖励模型提供启示？它对改进生成代码与现有代码库的集成有何潜力？

reason.en: The user is interested in RL from execution feedback and integration with existing codebases. The PTA references provide a structured representation of successful trajectories that could serve as a guide for training reward models to assess code generation quality beyond functional correctness.
reason.zh: 用户关注基于执行反馈的强化学习以及与现有代码库的集成。PTA参考提供了成功轨迹的结构化表示，可作为训练奖励模型以评估超越功能正确性的代码生成质量的指南。

answer.en:
The PTA references constructed by AgentLens, which merge multiple passing solutions to capture process-level patterns, can directly inform reward model design by providing structured representations of successful trajectories beyond mere functional correctness. For instance, the quality score that separates trajectories into "Lucky, Solid, and Ideal tiers" and further decomposes Lucky Passes into "five recurring mechanisms" such as blind retries and missing verification could serve as a multi-faceted reward signal. Training a reward model to predict these quality components would encourage agents to avoid chaotic trial-and-error behaviors and instead follow the orderly Exploration→Implementation→Verification patterns encoded in the PTA references. The context-sensitive intent labeling — assigning actions to "Exploration, Implementation, Verification, or Orchestration based on trajectory history" — offers a natural process-level curriculum for reinforcement learning from execution feedback.

For integration with existing codebases, the PTA references capture a consensus of diverse, successful patches for the same task, highlighting action sequences that reliably lead to correct integration. By learning to align with these prototypical trajectories, agents can reduce waste (e.g., blind retries) and increase the likelihood that their patches adhere to established workflows, improving compatibility with existing code. The release of "task-level Prefix Tree Acceptor (PTA) references" alongside "waste signals, divergence points" provides concrete data to train models that prioritize structured, context-aware code generation over lucky guesses, which is critical for reliable integration in real software projects.
answer.zh:
AgentLens构建的PTA参考通过合并多个通过解来捕获过程级模式，可直接为奖励模型设计提供信息，通过提供超越功能正确性的成功轨迹结构化表示。例如，将轨迹分为'Lucky、Solid和Ideal等级'并进一步将Lucky Pass分解为'五种反复出现的机制'（如盲目重试和缺失验证）的质量分数可作为多方面的奖励信号。训练奖励模型预测这些质量成分将鼓励智能体避免混乱的试错行为，转而遵循PTA参考中编码的有序探索→实施→验证模式。上下文敏感的意图标记——根据轨迹历史将动作分配到'探索、实施、验证或编排'——为基于执行反馈的强化学习提供了自然的过程级课程。

对于与现有代码库的集成，PTA参考捕获了同一任务不同成功补丁的共识，突出了可靠导致正确集成的动作序列。通过学习与这些原型轨迹对齐，智能体可以减少浪费（如盲目重试），并提高其补丁遵循既定工作流的可能性，从而提升与现有代码的兼容性。'任务级前缀树接受器（PTA）参考'以及'浪费信号、分歧点'的发布为训练模型提供了具体数据，这些模型优先考虑结构化、上下文感知的代码生成而非幸运猜测，这对于真实软件项目中的可靠集成至关重要。
